pages.text: Novus builds its weather from three mods working at once, replacing the flat grey sheet vanilla calls a storm.$(br2)$(li)$(bold)Simple Clouds$() draws real, volumetric clouds overhead and grows them into $(italic)localized storms$() — a squall can be raining on your field and not on the hill behind it.$(li)$(bold)Particle Rain$() makes the rain and snow you see fall, and carries the weather's sound — the hush and gust of it.$(li)$(bold)Serene Seasons$() decides how $(italic)often$() weather comes, by the turning year.
pages.title: Three Hands

pages.text: $(bold)Storms are local now.$() Because Simple Clouds runs its weather per-place rather than across the whole world at once, you can watch a storm approach across the landscape and walk out the far side of it. This is the look Novus is after — sky you can read.$(br2)It also means the old vanilla habit of $(italic)"is it raining: yes/no"$() no longer describes the sky. The world's global weather flag stays clear even while a Simple Clouds storm pours on your roof — which matters for the redstone on the next page.
pages.title: Storms Are Local
